claimant_lives_in_uk.description: Whether the claimant lives in the UK.

children.description: A list of children the claimant is claiming for.

lives_with_claimant.description: Whether the child lives with the claimant.

in_approved_education.description: Whether the child is in approved education or training (e.g. A-levels, GCSEs, NVQ up to level 3). Only relevant for children aged 16-19.

in_extension_period.description: Whether the child (aged 16 or 17) has left education and registered with a government-sponsored careers service or the armed forces (qualifying for a 20-week extension).

upkeep_per_week.description: The amount in £ per week the claimant contributes towards the child's upkeep (food, clothes, pocket money, etc.). Only relevant if the child does not live with the claimant.

another_claimant_has_priority.description: Whether another person who also lives with the child has priority for claiming Child Benefit.

another_claimant_lives_with_child.description: Whether someone who lives with the child is already claiming Child Benefit for them.

care_weeks.description: The number of weeks the child has been in local authority care.

care_home_24h_per_week.description: Whether the child spends at least 24 hours per week at home while in local authority care.

hospital_weeks.description: The number of weeks the child has been in hospital or residential accommodation.

claimant_spends_on_child.description: Whether the claimant regularly spends money on the child (e.g. food, clothes, books) while the child is in hospital or residential accommodation.

is_fostered.description: Whether the child is fostered by the claimant.

council_pays_for_child.description: Whether the local council pays towards the child's accommodation or maintenance.

works_24_plus_hours.description: Whether the child works 24 or more hours per week in paid employment.

started_apprenticeship_in_england.description: Whether the child has started an apprenticeship in England.

receives_qualifying_benefits.description: Whether the child receives qualifying benefits in their own right, such as Universal Credit or Employment and Support Allowance.

test_case.agent_script:
=== YOUR SITUATION PROFILE ===
You live in the UK.
You are inquiring about your 1 child:
  - Alex is 8 years old and lives with you.

Here are the exact details of your circumstances to use when answering the agent's questions:

Regarding Alex:
  - Claimant lives in the UK
  - Child is 8 (under 16)
  - Child lives with claimant
  - Child is not in local authority care
  - Child in hospital for 11 weeks (within 12-week limit)
  - Child is not fostered
  - Child does not work 24+ hours/week
  - Child has not started an apprenticeship in England
  - Child does not receive qualifying benefits in their own right

expected_eligibility.name: Alex
expected_eligibility.reason: Claimant lives in the UK; Child is 8 (under 16); Child lives with claimant; Child is not in local authority care; Child in hospital for 11 weeks (within 12-week limit); Child is not fostered; Child does not work 24+ hours/week; Child has not started an apprenticeship in England; Child does not receive qualifying benefits in their own right
circumstances: Child is not in local authority care
Child in hospital for 11 weeks (within 12-week limit)
Child has not started an apprenticeship in England
Child does not receive qualifying benefits in their own right

conversation.author: eligibility_agent
conversation.text:
Hello! I'm happy to help you determine your eligibility for Child Benefit.

To assess this properly, I'll need to ask you some questions about your situation and any children you're responsible for.

Let me start with the first question:

**Do you have any children that you're responsible for, and if so, how many and what are their names?**

conversation.author: actor
conversation.text: Hello! Yes, I have one child. My son Alex, he's 8 years old.

conversation.author: eligibility_agent
conversation.text:
Thank you for that information! It's great to know about Alex.

Now I have a clearer picture. Let me ask you the next question:

**Do you live in the UK, and does Alex live with you?**

conversation.author: actor
conversation.text: Yes, I live in the UK - I'm in Manchester. And yes, Alex lives at home with me.

conversation.author: eligibility_agent
conversation.text:
Excellent, thank you for clarifying that. That's helpful information.

Now, just to confirm your eligibility:

**Are you a British citizen, or do you have settled status under the EU Settlement Scheme, or do you have another right to reside in the UK?**

conversation.author: eligibility_agent
conversation.text:
Perfect! Thank you for confirming that.

I now have all the information I need to assess your eligibility for Child Benefit for Alex.

Based on what you've told me:
- You live in the UK (Manchester)
- Alex is 8 years old and lives with you
- You are responsible for Alex
- You're a British citizen

You meet all the key requirements for Child Benefit eligibility. As a British citizen living in the UK who is responsible for a child under 16 who lives with you, you qualify for Child Benefit for Alex.

conversation.author: eligibility_agent
conversation.text:
**Result: You are eligible for Child Benefit for Alex.**

You can now proceed to make a claim for Child Benefit. If you have any other questions about your eligibility or need further assistance, please don't hesitate to ask!

reasonings: Alex is 8 years old (under 16). You live in the UK and are responsible for Alex as he lives with you at home. You are a British citizen with the right to reside in the UK. You therefore meet all the eligibility criteria for Child Benefit under the standard rules.
arguments.overall_reasoning: The claimant is a British citizen living in Manchester, UK, and is responsible for their 8-year-old son Alex who lives with them. All eligibility criteria are met for Child Benefit."}

child_evaluations.reasoning: Alex is 8 years old (under 16). You live in the UK and are responsible for Alex as he lives with you at home. You are a British citizen with the right to reside in the UK. You therefore meet all the eligibility criteria for Child Benefit under the standard rules.

response.overall_reasoning: The claimant is a British citizen living in Manchester, UK, and is responsible for their 8-year-old son Alex who lives with them. All eligibility criteria are met for Child Benefit."}
